title_en: AI Tool Discovery at Scale: All You Need is DNS
summary_zh: 随着自主AI代理时代的到来，需求一种能够高效导航数百万工具的发现机制。现有方案因O(N)复杂度和集中治理而受限。本文提出ToolDNS，一种将语义工具发现嵌入互联网最稳健的基础设施——域名系统（DNS）的全新框架。通过在分层命名空间中嵌入功能意图和组织信任，将昂贵的语义搜索转化为轻量的O(log N)名称解析。引入部分展开名称、EDNS0意图负载和逻辑子域等三项协议增强，实现分散治理和语义剪枝。借助涵盖33,688个现实工具的异构基准测试，ToolDNS将查询空间缩减95.26%，实现与最先进检索精度相当的性能，且UDP原生设计使发现延迟大幅降低。此工作表明，实现可扩展AI互操作性，关键在于更智能地利用现有基础设施，而非增加中间件。
summary_en: The upcoming era of autonomous AI agents requires a discovery mechanism capable of efficiently navigating millions of tools. Existing solutions suffer from O(N) complexity and centralized control. The paper proposes ToolDNS, a novel framework embedding semantic tool discovery into the resilient Domain Name System (DNS). By embedding functional intent and organizational trust into a hierarchical namespace, it converts costly semantic searches into lightweight O(log N) name resolutions. Three protocol-compliant enhancements—partially unfolded names, EDNS0 intent payloads, and logical subdomains—enable decentralized governance and semantic pruning. Evaluated on a large heterogeneous benchmark of 33,688 real tools, ToolDNS reduces per-query search space by 95.26%, matches state-of-the-art retrieval accuracy, and offers greatly reduced latency through its UDP-native design. This demonstrates that scalable AI interoperability depends on smarter use of existing infrastructure rather than adding middleware.
why_it_matters_zh: ToolDNS利用域名系统的稳定性和层级结构，有效降低了大规模AI工具发现的复杂度和延迟，提升了检索效率与准确率，解决了当前方案中规模和治理难题，推动AI生态更高效互操作。
why_it_matters_en: ToolDNS leverages the robustness and hierarchy of DNS to significantly reduce complexity and latency in large-scale AI tool discovery, improving retrieval efficiency and accuracy. It addresses scalability and governance challenges in existing methods, thereby advancing more efficient AI ecosystem interoperability.
image_prompt: Modern editorial square illustration for an AI news app. Topic: AI Tool Discovery at Scale: All You Need is DNS. Category: image_ai. Clean, premium, technology-focused, no text, no logos.
ranking_reason: Selected from authoritative primary source; quality score 82.

title_zh: 基于分布式反馈控制的无人机运动角度稳定的积分微分方程研究
title_en: Integro-Differential Equations in Angular Stabilization of Drone Motion by Distributed Feedback Control
summary_zh: 本文提出了一种利用积分算子的分布式反馈控制方法，实现无人机运动角度的稳定。该积分算子具有无限记忆特性，借助长期观测数据构建更优控制策略。文章发展了一种普适方法，将含无限记忆的积分微分方程稳定性问题转化为常微分方程系统的分析，针对线性近似研究了具有有限方程数量的系统，且通过复杂核的组合显著增强稳定效果。最后将理论成果应用于无人机飞行稳定性控制，获得了令人意外的指数稳定性结果。
summary_en: This paper proposes angular stabilization of drone motion using distributed feedback control represented by an integral operator with potentially unbounded memory. Leveraging extended observation times enables better control strategies based on past states. The authors develop a universal approach that reduces stability analysis of integro-differential equations with unbounded memory to studying systems of ordinary differential equations, possibly infinite-dimensional. Under linear approximation, the problem simplifies to systems with finite equations using exponential kernels, with more complex kernel combinations improving stabilization. The new exponential stability results are applied to drone flight stabilization.
why_it_matters_zh: 该研究突破了传统积分微分控制理论的限制，处理了积分算子无限记忆的复杂性，提供了将复杂动态稳定性问题转化为常微分方程系统的新方法，具有重要理论价值和实际意义，尤其在无人机精确角度控制领域展现出广阔应用前景。
why_it_matters_en: This research overcomes the limitations of classical integro-differential control by addressing unbounded memory effects, introducing a novel framework that converts complex stability problems into ordinary differential equation analyses. Its theoretical advances have practical implications, notably enhancing precise angular control in drones, thereby promising improvements in aerial robotics stability.
image_prompt: Modern editorial square illustration for an AI news app. Topic: Integro-Differential Equations in Angular Stabilization of Drone Motion by Distributed Feedback Control. Category: research_papers. Clean, premium, technology-focused, no text, no logos.
ranking_reason: Selected from authoritative primary source; quality score 58.

title_zh: 构建欧洲多语言评估数据集：EMT网络中的MMLU本地化项目
title_en: Building a European Multilingual Evaluation Dataset: The MMLU Localisation Project within the EMT Network
summary_zh: 本文报道了欧盟翻译总司（DGT）与欧洲翻译硕士（EMT）合作，将MMLU数据集本地化为11种欧洲语言。该项目不仅创建了更具包容性的LLM评估基准，还为硕士生提供了真实的项目基础专业训练，涵盖翻译、修订、项目管理和多语言协调，同时指出了关键的技术、管理及工作流程挑战。
summary_en: This paper reports on a collaboration between the Directorate-General for Translation (DGT) and the European Master's in Translation (EMT) to localise the MMLU dataset into 11 European languages. The project creates a more inclusive benchmark for large language model evaluation and offers master's students authentic training in translation, revision, project management, and multilingual coordination, while highlighting key methodological, administrative, and workflow challenges.
why_it_matters_zh: 该项目通过多语言数据集的构建促进了大语言模型评估的多样性和包容性，同时为翻译硕士提供宝贵的实战经验，有助于培养未来的多语言翻译和管理人才。
why_it_matters_en: The project enhances diversity and inclusiveness in large language model evaluation by building a multilingual dataset, while providing valuable hands-on experience for translation master's students, helping to train future multilingual translation and management professionals.
image_prompt: Modern editorial square illustration for an AI news app. Topic: Building a European Multilingual Evaluation Dataset: The MMLU Localisation Project within the EMT Network. Category: research_papers. Clean, premium, technology-focused, no text, no logos.

title_zh: 具有灵活生成意义和表达备选方案的语用推理计算模型
title_en: Computational Models of Pragmatic Reasoning with Flexible Generation of Meaning and Expression Alternatives
summary_zh: 本文提出了一种名为SAGE（ScAffolded Generative models for Explanation）的框架，将认知模型的解释透明性与语言模型的生成灵活性相结合，用于模拟语用推理过程。SAGE将语用过程分解为三类模块：提议者（利用语言模型生成候选备选），评估者（对备选方案进行语义、复杂度或典型性评估），以及选择者（执行基于认知任务分析的规则步骤）。通过三个案例研究评估该模型，涵盖指称表达生成、方式含义和Grice会话含义，结果显示SAGE在准确性上优于基线模型。研究还发现语言模型在生成备选方案方面表现优越，但在评估理论性或形式性测度任务时效果较弱。文中讨论了神经符号模型在解释人类语用语言使用上的潜力与局限性。
summary_en: This paper introduces SAGE (ScAffolded Generative models for Explanation), a framework combining the explanatory transparency of cognitive models with the generative flexibility of language models to simulate pragmatic reasoning. SAGE decomposes the pragmatic process into proposers (generating candidate alternatives via language models), evaluators (assessing semantics, complexity, or typicality), and selectors (executing rule-based cognitive task steps). Evaluated through three case studies involving referential expression generation, manner implicatures, and Gricean conversational implicatures, SAGE demonstrated higher accuracy compared to baselines. Results reveal language models excel at generating alternatives but are less effective at theoretical/formal evaluation tasks. The paper discusses the promise and limitations of neuro-symbolic models as explanatory accounts of human pragmatic language use.
why_it_matters_zh: 该研究创新性地结合了认知模型和语言模型，弥补了传统语用模型中人工指定备选集的局限，提升了模型的灵活性和应用范围。通过系统评估，展示了其在模拟人类语用推理中的有效性和潜力，为自然语言理解和生成研究提供了新的方法论视角。
why_it_matters_en: This research innovatively integrates cognitive models with language models, overcoming the limitations of manually specified alternative sets in traditional pragmatics models, enhancing flexibility and applicability. Systematic evaluation demonstrates its effectiveness and potential in simulating human pragmatic reasoning, offering a novel methodological perspective for natural language understanding and generation studies.
image_prompt: Modern editorial square illustration for an AI news app. Topic: Computational Models of Pragmatic Reasoning with Flexible Generation of Meaning and Expression Alternatives. Category: research_papers. Clean, premium, technology-focused, no text, no logos.
